title: oxybaphus nyctagineus var. pilosus
intro: Oxybaphus nyctagineus var. pilosus, also known as a hairy four o'clock, is a variety of flowering plant in the evening primrose family that is native to North America. The hairy four o'clock has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. This plant is believed to have health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems, making it a valuable herb in herbal remedies. Hairy four o'clock can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
